segments.s082-g73: شد is a past-change verb 'became/was' placed before the noun phrase to announce a change of state (here implying his passing); آن is the demonstrative determiner 'that' introducing the following epithets; نامور and پرهنر are two attributive adjectives ('renowned' and 'full of art/skill') that stack before شهریار, the head noun 'king'. The sequence places emphasis on the king by fronting the verb and piling epithets before the title.
segments.s082-g74: به گیتی is a locative prepositional phrase ('in the world') with به used in its 'in/within' sense; سخن ماند is the main clause where ماند is the past intransitive 'remained/lasted' and سخن ('words/poems') is its subject—i.e. his speech/poetry endured. ز + (ـو) is the poetic compound meaning 'from him' (ز = 'from'), and یادگار is a noun 'memorial/memento' showing that what remained served as his remembrance.
segments.s082-g9: This is a compact adverbial clause: گه (an occasional/poetic 'if/when') + a nominalized action رفتن ('going' here = dying) followed by آمد ('comes/arrives'); the prepositional phrase به دیگر سرای ('to another abode') marks the destination. Word order is literary: the event noun + آمد expresses 'when the going comes' = 'when death arrives'.
segments.s082-g10: This is a wish/optative clause introduced by مگر (here used poetically to introduce a hopeful wish), followed by نزد یزدان ('in the presence of God') and the verb phrase به آیدت جای ('may a place come to you' = 'may you find a place'). Note the archaic/poetic sequence where 'place' is the patient and آیدت is impersonal/optative in sense.
segments.s082-g11: Imperative نگر ('consider/look') begins the clause; تا introduces the subordinate content clause 'what', چه is the interrogative 'what' and governs the complement 'باید کنون ساختن' ('must now be done'). کنون is an adverb 'now' and ساختن here functions as a general verbal infinitive 'to do/perform' in the sense 'to undertake/arrange'.
segments.s082-g12: Negative impersonal نباید ('one must not' / 'it must not be') introduces a clause of consequence; که here works as 'that/because' linking to the reason clause 'مرگ آورد تاختن' (literally 'death brings charging' — i.e. 'charging will bring death'). آورد is causative 'brings/causes' and تاختن is the infinitive 'to charge/attack'.
segments.s082-g13: Temporal/subordinate clause: سخن ('words') is topical, چـون functions as a connective meaning 'when/as', ز داننده 'from the knower/sage' marks the source, بشنید is the past tense 'heard'. The subject شاه ('the king') is placed after the verb by poetic inversion, a common literary order.
segments.s082-g14: Prepositional phrase به رسم دگرگون modifies بیاراست گاه: به here is an adverbial/relational particle 'by/in' (introducing manner), رسم 'custom/rite', دگرگون 'changed/altered', and بیاراست is a past or perfective verb 'he adorned/arranged'. گاه is an adverbial noun 'occasion/time' closing the line. Together: 'he outfitted/arranged the occasion in a changed manner.'
segments.s082-g15: Direct action clause with object marker را: همه موبدان و ردان ('all the mobeds and the radax/elders') is the object, and بخواند is a past/habitual verb 'he called/summoned'. The particle را marks the definite object; word order is subject omitted (understood as the king) + object + verb.
segments.s082-g16: Complementary clause: همه راز دل ('all the secrets of the heart') is the object of a verb expressed by براند here, preceded by the locative/prepositional پیش ایشان ('before them/in their presence'). براند (from راندن) in this poetic use means 'to put/lay forth' or 'to disclose' rather than a literal 'drive away' — the clause says he disclosed all his heart's secrets to them.
segments.s082-g17: بفرمود is an honorific past-tense verb meaning 'he commanded/said'. تا here introduces a subordinate purpose/command clause ('so that' / 'that'), so تا + clause = 'that ...'. نوذر is the name Nowzar (direct object of the command clause). آمدش is آمد + ـش (ـش = 'to him/his/it') and is a simple past 'came to him'. پیش means 'in the presence of / before (someone)'; word order is object (Nowzar) then verb+clitic (آمدش) then adjunct/prepositional/prepositional-like 'پیش'. Together: 'He commanded that Nowzar come before him.'
segments.s082-g18: ورا is و + را or the older form meaning 'to him' (dative), marking the recipient of the advice. پندها is the plural of پند 'advice/admonition' (accent on plural). داد is the simple past 'gave'. ز here is the poetic preposition 'from / beyond' used in comparative phrases ('beyond/over the measure'). اندازه بیش is a noun phrase 'measure + excess' meaning 'excess of measure' or 'beyond the proper measure'. The whole phrase = 'he gave him admonitions about/exhorting him not to go beyond measure.'
segments.s082-g19: که introduces an explanatory clause 'for/that'. این تخت شاهی is a noun phrase 'this royal throne' (این = this, تخت = throne, شاهی = regal/kingship). فسونست is an archaic/poetic copula + predicate meaning 'is enchantment' (فسون = enchantment, -ست = 'is'). و باد places an optative particle باد after 'and' meaning 'may it.../let it be...' (a wish/optative). So this segment states: 'that this royal throne is enchantment, and (may)...'.
segments.s082-g20: برو here is a contracted classical sequence (literally 'بر او' = 'upon it' or 'on that'); it does not mean the imperative 'go' but rather 'on it / to it' in older usage (this is why a modern dictionary sense 'go' does not fit exactly). جاودان is an adjective 'eternal'. دل is the subject/thing of attachment 'heart'. نباید is the impersonal/modal 'one must not / should not'. نهاد is the past form of نهادن 'to place/put' (here passive/impersonal idea: 'to set/place one's heart'). The clause as a whole: 'one should not place the heart on it as eternal.'
segments.s082-g21: This is a classical Persian idiom for stating age. The construction [X را بر N سالیان شد] means 'X became N years old.' مرا (here split as م + را in the data) is the dative/patient 'to me / I' (the one affected). بر صد و بیست means 'to/at one hundred and twenty' (بر used in age expressions), شد is the past of شدن 'became', and سالیان is the plural 'years'. Together: 'I became one hundred and twenty years (old).'
segments.s082-g22: به رنج و به سختی is a paired prepositional phrase 'by/with toil and with hardship' (repetition of به for emphasis). ببستم is a poetic/past form of بستن here used in the sense 'I spent / I was bound by / I endured' (the precise lexical list supplied does not include this nuance, so it is treated as a contextual past of 'to bear/undergo'). میان means 'among / in the midst'. The clause: 'I (then) endured them amid toil and hardship' or simply 'I spent (those years) in toil and hardship.'
segments.s082-g23: بسی is an intensifier 'much/very' (classical usage). شادی و کام دل is a coordinated object: شادی 'joy' and کام دل 'the heart's desire/fulfillment' (کام takes possessive/genitive relation with دل). راندم is the simple past 'I drove away / I expelled'. The verb takes the preceding noun phrase as its object: 'I drove away much joy and the heart's satisfaction.'
segments.s082-g24: به رزم introduces the circumstance 'into/for battle' (به + noun). اندرون 'inside / within' combines with دشمنان 'enemies' to mean 'within the enemies' ranks/among the enemy'. ماندم is the past 'I remained / I stayed'. Together: 'I remained among the enemies in battle.'
segments.s082-g49: پدید آید is a two-word verbal phrase meaning ‘will appear/come forth’; پدید (appear) is placed before the verb آید (comes) in a poetic/VSO-friendly order. آنگه (= then) sets the temporal frame, and به خاور زمین is a prepositional phrase ('toward/in the eastern land') with به marking direction/place.
segments.s082-g50: نگر is an imperative ('look!/behold!'). تا introduces a purpose/result clause ('so that' / 'in order that') and is followed by the subjunctive/present form نتابی here meaning 'you do not turn/bend' (negative notion incorporated in the form). بر او = 'against him' (بر = against; او = him), and بر به کین is an older/prepositional phrasing meaning 'into/with hatred' (کین = hatred); the double prepositions reflect archaic poetic syntax rather than two separate actions.
segments.s082-g51: بدو is the prepositional pronoun 'to him/unto him'. بگرو is an archaic imperative/command meaning 'take/accept' (here 'give him/accept for him'); آن دین is 'that religion/faith' and یزدان بود is a past copula construction meaning 'was of God' (یزدان = God; بود = was).
segments.s082-g52: نگه کن is an imperative phrase 'look/observe' (نگه + the imperative کن). ز سر is a prepositional phrase 'from the head/from the start' (ز = from; سر = head/start). تا introduces a subordinate clause meaning 'to see/so that' and is followed by چه پیمان بود 'what covenant there was' (چه = what; پیمان = covenant; بود = was).
segments.s082-g53: تو is the emphatic subject 'you'; مگذار is a negative imperative ('do not allow / do not leave / do not abandon'). هرگز is an adverb 'never' modifying the verb. ره ایزدی is the direct object 'the divine way/path' (ره = way; ایزدی = divine), object following the verb in this poetic order.
segments.s082-g54: که introduces an explanatory clause ('for/that'). نیکی ازویست is an idiomatic compressed phrase: ازویست = از وی است ('is from him'), so 'goodness is from Him.' و هم زو بدی repeats the same structure for contrast ('and likewise from Him [is] evil'); the poetic contraction زو stands for از او ('from him').
segments.s082-g55: ازان پس is a temporal phrase 'after that' (ازان = from that; پس = then/after). بیاید is a third-person form used prophetically/future-wise ('will come' / 'shall come'). ز ترکان سپاه is the origin + subject: 'a host/army from the Turks' (ز = from; ترکان = the Turks; سپاه = army/host).
segments.s082-g56: نهند is the third-person plural classical form of 'place/put' (they place/they will place). از بر تخت is an older prepositional combination meaning 'on/upon the throne' (از بر here indicating position atop), ایران names the realm, and کلاه (literally 'hat') is the object — a figurative/cultural image of placing a cap/crown on the Iranian throne.
segments.s082-g1: منوچهر را is a topical/accusative phrase (را marks the person whose years are being counted). سال شد is a fixed poetic copular construction 'years became' = 'his years amounted to…'. بر before the numeral phrase indicates the measure/amount 'to/amounting to'.
segments.s082-g2: ز گیتی = 'from the world' (ز = from). همی is a literary/progressive particle marking ongoing or immediate action. بار رفتن = 'the burden/responsibility of going' (بار here = 'burden'); رفتن in this context has the idiomatic sense 'to depart/leave (life)'. ببست is a past tense verb meaning 'he bound up / closed up' (literary: finished/tied up the affair).
segments.s082-g3: ستاره‌شناسان is the plural noun 'astrologers/stargazers'. بر او شدند is a literary way of saying 'they turned toward/attended to him' — بر + pronoun plus شدند (past plural of شدن) expresses 'set themselves upon / became directed to him'.
segments.s082-g4: همی starts the following action (progressive/ongoing). ز آسمان داستانها زدند literally 'they struck/took stories from the sky'; here زدند is used in the sense 'uttered/performed/told' (they told tales that come from the heavens) and داستانها = 'stories/tales'.
segments.s082-g5: ندیدند is simple past negative 'they did not see'. روزش is 'his day' (روز + possessive suffix -ش). کشیدن دراز is a verbal phrase where کشیدن 'to draw' takes the adjective دراز 'long' to mean 'to be drawn/prolonged' (i.e., 'to last long').
segments.s082-g6: ز گیتی = 'from the world'. همی گشت is a literary/periphrastic past 'it came to pass / it turned out'. بایست is modal 'must/ought' (literary 'it had to be'), and باز is adverb 'again' or 'back' — together indicating that he was required to leave the world once more.
segments.s082-g7: بدادند (past plural) 'they gave' or 'they delivered'. زان روز تلخ literally 'from that bitter day' (زان = 'from that' / 'on that'), and آگهی = 'news/notice/information' — the phrase means they announced bitter news on that day.
segments.s082-g8: که introduces the content clause 'that ...'. شد تیره = 'became dark/ominous' (شد = 'became', تیره here = 'dark/ominous'). آن تخت شاهنشهی = 'that throne of kingship/imperial throne' (آن determinative + تخت 'throne' + شاهنشهی 'imperial rule/sovereignty').
segments.s082-g57: ترا is the older/clitic form equivalent to تو را ('to you' / 'for you') placed at the start for emphasis. کارهای درشتست is a compacted predicate where درشتست = درشت است ('is grave/large') — classical Persian often fuses the copula -است to the adjective. پیش here means 'ahead'/'before' (i.e. 'before you' or 'in store for you'), so the whole segment reads 'Grave matters are before you.'
segments.s082-g58: گهی is an adverb 'sometimes/now and then'. The clause is elliptical: 'گهی گرگ باید بدن گاه میش' — باید is a modal 'must/ought to'. Here the subject is گرگ ('a wolf'); بدن 'the body' is used as a predicative noun, and گاه میش ('at times, a ewe') modifies that predication. In classical Persian the copula 'باشد' can be omitted, so the sense is 'sometimes a wolf must (be) in the body/of the guise of a ewe' (i.e., must act like a sheep).
segments.s082-g59: گزند ('harm') is the nominative subject; تو ('to you') marks the affected person. آید is the present/future sense 'will come' (classical narrative uses present to mean 'will happen'). ز 'from' introduces the source: پور پشنگ = 'the son (pur) Pashang' (a name/epithet). Word order is topical: source follows the verb for emphasis.
segments.s082-g60: ز توران 'from/because of Turan' sets the cause or source. شود is the copular/verb 'become/happen' (here 'will become'); کارها 'matters/affairs' is the grammatical subject; بر تو ننگ = 'a shame upon you' where بر = 'upon/on' and ننگ = 'shame'. Classical Persian often allows this V‑order with شود following the agent/circumstance.
segments.s082-g61: بجوی is an exhortation/imperative 'seek!' (classical present-imperative form). ای پسر is the vocative 'O son'. چون رسد داوری is a temporal subordinate clause: چون here functions as 'when/once' (classical usage), رسد is 'arrives/comes', and داوری = 'the judgment' — together: 'seek, O son, when judgment comes.'
segments.s082-g62: ز سام و ز زال 'from Sam and from Zal' names the sources of assistance. آنگهی ('thereupon/then') points to the time immediately after the judgment arrives, and یاوری is the object 'help/aid' (the thing to seek). Syntactically this continues the imperative بجوی: 'seek then help from Sam and Zal.'
segments.s082-g63: وزین here is an archaic/poetic contraction equivalent to و ز این ('and from this'); older epic Persian often fuses such particles. نو درختی = 'a new tree' (درختی used as a noun 'tree'), and که از پشت زال is a relative clause 'that from Zal's back...' — so the segment sets up 'and from this new tree, which (came) from Zal's back'.
segments.s082-g64: برآمد is a past/simple perfect 'arose/issued forth'. کنون 'now' follows to mark the present result, and برکشد (from برکشیدن) is used here in the sense 'it draws up/puts forth' (present/factual). شاخ و یال = 'branch and mane' (شاخ as bough/branch here, یال as 'mane' or poetic crest). The clause describes the tree's having now put forth branch and mane.
segments.s082-g41: This is a noun phrase with a relative clause: نشانی (a sign/trace) is modified by که ماند همی (that will/did endure constantly). همی is a poetic continuous marker emphasing ongoingness; از تو (literally 'from you') gives the origin/possession ('of you'). باز at the end of the segment is an adverb 'again' placed for emphasis before the verb in the next segment.
segments.s082-g42: برآید is third-person singular (present/subjunctive or future-oriented) of برآمدن 'to appear/arise' — here 'will/does appear'. برو is the imperative 'go' used poetically as an address or interjection toward time; روزگار دراز is a noun phrase 'long time' (adjective دراز follows روزگار). Together the segment says that the trace will reappear as/when the long time proceeds.
segments.s082-g43: نباید is an impersonal modal 'it should not / ought not'; it takes a subordinate clause introduced by که plus the subjunctive/inflected باشد 'be'. جز از means 'except from'; آفرین here functions poetically as 'the Creator' (the present-stem form of 'to create') rather than an interjection.
segments.s082-g44: که introduces an explanatory clause ('for'/'that'). پاکی نژاد is a genitive-like phrase 'purity of lineage/lineage purity' (پاکی modifies نژاد); آورد is third-person singular 'brings/causes to come about' and پاک دین is 'pure religion' (adjective before noun here). The clause says that pure birth/lineage brings (or results in) pure religion.
segments.s082-g45: نگر is an imperative 'look!/take heed!'. تا here introduces a purposive/negative-clause ('so that' / 'lest') and is followed by نتابی, the negative present/subjunctive/optative of تابیدن 'to turn/bend' (ن‑ negates the verb), meaning 'you do not turn away'. ز is the poetic form of از 'from', and دین خدای is 'the religion of God' (noun followed by its possessor).
segments.s082-g46: که here gives the reason/explanation 'for/because': دین خدای (repeated noun phrase 'God's religion') is the subject, آورد is 'brings' (third-person singular), and پاک رای is a compound meaning 'pure counsel/plan' (پاک qualifying رای). The whole clause explains why one should not turn away.
segments.s082-g47: کنون 'now' sets temporal focus; نو شود is the verbal phrase 'becomes new' where شود is the third-person singular subjunctive/passive of شدن used prospectively ('will be made/new will arise'). در جهان is a locative phrase 'in the world' and داوری is the noun 'judgment' (object/topic). Word order and verb mood give a prophetic/future sense.
segments.s082-g48: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when'; موسی is the proper name Moses; بیاید is a present/subjunctive form 'comes/arrives' (here future-oriented); به پیغمبری literally 'to/into prophethood' means 'as a prophet' — a prepositional phrase marking the capacity in which he comes.
segments.s082-g25: به + noun phrase marks the indirect/locative relation: 'به فر فریدون' = 'to/into the farr of Fereydun' (noun‑noun juxtaposition shows possession: فرِ فریدون). ببستم is a 1st‑person past verb ('I bound/placed/closed'); the doubled b- is an archaic/poetic form of the past. میان is an adverbial/locative ('between/in the midst') placed after the verb for emphasis, so the whole unit reads 'I placed/bound Fereydun's farr in the midst.'
segments.s082-g26: به پندش is an ablative/causal phrase 'by his advice' (پند + enclitic ـش = 'his advice'); مرا is the object 'to me' (written here as م + را: م = 'me' enclitic, را = object/ topicalizing marker). سود شد is a light‑noun + verb construction: 'sud / profit + became' = 'profit arose/wascome'; the clause ends with the contrasted noun phrase هر زیان ('every harm/loss') placed after the predicate in a poetic inversion: 'every loss became profit (for me)'.
segments.s082-g27: بجستم is an archaic/past 1st‑person verb meaning roughly 'I withdrew/escaped/removed myself' (poetic past form). ز ... و ز is the repeated preposition 'from ... and from ...' introducing the sources 'سلم' and 'تور'. سترگ follows and functions as a noun/adjectival element 'sight/gaze' (i.e. 'from the sight/gaze of Salm and of Tur'), so the whole phrase means 'I withdrew from Salm and from Tur's sight.'
segments.s082-g28: همان is a demonstrative 'that/the same' linking back to the previous clause. کین is the nominal agent 'hatred/vengeance.' The following noun phrase 'ایرج نیای بزرگ' is appositive: 'Iraj, the great ancestor' — i.e. 'that same hatred (concerned) Iraj, the great ancestor.' In this construction the apposition identifies whose hatred is meant.
segments.s082-g29: جهان (the world) is the direct object of کردم 'I made/did.' ویژه is an adjective meaning 'special/distinct' placed before the verb in a causative construction 'I made the world special/separate.' ز + plural noun (ز پتیاره‌ها) marks separation/source 'from the پتیاره‑ha' — an archaic/obscure plural; the preposition shows what the world is set apart from.
segments.s082-g30: This is a paired, repetitive structure for emphasis. بسی (here 'many/much') modifies 'شهر کردم' = 'I made/founded cities' (کردم = 1st‑person past 'I did/made'). The second half 'بسی باره‌ها' repeats the quantifier with باره‌ها ('occasions/instances') to mean 'many times/ on many occasions.' The sequence is idiomatic poetic repetition: 'I made many cities, many times.'
segments.s082-g31: چنانم = 'it is such with me / I am such' (چنان + enclitic ـم). که introduces a subordinate clause. گویی here is a modal particle meaning 'as if / it seems' (not the literal 'speech' sense), and ندیدم is the 1st‑person past negative 'I did not see.' جهان is the object; together the clause reads '...that, as if, I had not seen the world.'
segments.s082-g32: شمار 'the count/number' is subject. گذشته شد is a compound meaning 'has passed/has gone over' (گذشته = past/gone + شد = became/happened), so 'the count has passed.' اندر نهان is an adverbial phrase 'into/within concealment' (اندر = in/into, نهان = hidden). The segment thus says the reckoning/tally 'has passed into concealment.'
segments.s082-g33: نیرزد is an impersonal/third-person classical present negative verb meaning "is not worth." همی is an archaic/emphatic adverbial particle here reinforcing the verb (roughly "indeed/at all"). زندگانیش is the noun زندگان (living/life) with the possessive enclitic -ش "his/its," so "his life." مرگ is the nominal complement ("the death") that the verb evaluates: literally "his life is not worth (the) death ...".
segments.s082-g34: درختی is an indefinite noun "a tree." که is the relative pronoun "that/which" introducing the relative clause. زهر آورد is a verb phrase: زهر ("poison") is the object/qualifier and آورد (past/perfective third-person singular of آوردن) means "brings/produces/puts forth." بار و برگ are the items affected — بار (here: produce/fruit or load) and برگ ("leaf"). Word order is poetic: verb (آورد) follows its object (زهر) and then the objects (بار و برگ).
segments.s082-g35: ازان پس که is a temporal clause marker "after that, when/that." بردم is first-person past of بردن here meaning "I bore/underwent/ endured" (I took upon me); بسی is an adverb "much/very"; درد و رنج are the coordinated nouns "pain and suffering/hardship." The clause as a whole gives the time/background for what follows.
segments.s082-g36: سپردم is first-person past "I handed over/entrusted." ترا is the combination of the second-person clitic (ت) plus the object marker (را) and functions as the indirect object "to you" (i.e. "I entrusted to you"). تخت شاهی is a noun phrase "throne (تخت) of kingship (شاهی)" and و گنج adds "and treasure." The verb precedes the objects in this poetic ordering but the meaning is standard: "I entrusted to you the royal throne and treasure."
segments.s082-g37: چنان چون together form a doubled comparative/particle pair meaning "just as/so as." فریدون is the subject (proper name). مرا is split in the line as م + را; grammatically this is the object "to me/me" (م = me, را = object marker). داده بود is the pluperfect/past-perfect compound (داده = past participle "given," بود as auxiliary) meaning "had given." The clause thus says "just as Fereydun had given (it) to me."
segments.s082-g38: ترا again appears split (ت = second-person clitic, را = object marker) yielding "to you." دادم is first-person past "I gave." این تاج شاه آزمود is the direct object: این تاج ("this crown"), شاه (attrib. "of kingship/kingly"), آزمود (past participle "tried/tested"). Word order places the indirect object before the verb and the demonstrative + noun phrase after it in a normal Persian object structure rendered here in poetic syntax.
segments.s082-g39: چنان دان is an imperative or hortative phrase meaning "consider it so / know it thus" (چنان = thus; دان = know/consider). که introduces the content clause "that ..." خوردی here uses خوردن in the idiomatic/archaic sense "to befall" or "to suffer; to happen to" and is second-person past "you suffered/befell you." و بر تو گذشت is coordinated: بر ("upon/on/over") + تو ("you") + گذشت (past "passed") = "and it passed over you / it is past for you."
segments.s082-g40: به خوشتر زمان is a prepositional phrase "to a better time" (به = to/toward, خوشتر = comparative "happier/better", زمان = "time"). بازم is the adverbial element meaning "again/once more" in the sense "back/return" (here written together in the line); بایدت is the modal "it is necessary" or "you must" with the enclitic -ت addressing "you." گشت is the main verb (third-person/past form of گشتن used here in a modal/result sense) so the sequence بایدت گشت yields "you must be turned/you must return/be restored." Together: "you must be returned to a happier time."
segments.s082-g65: ازو is a prepositional/pronominal adverb meaning 'because of him' or 'from him' and introduces the cause. شهر توران is a noun phrase with شهر 'city' as head and توران as a specifier ('the city of Turan'). شود is the present/future third‑person singular of شدن 'become' and takes بی‌هنر as its predicative complement, so the clause literally reads 'from him the city of Turan becomes unskilled.'
segments.s082-g66: به کین تو آید is a verb phrase where به کین ('with/for vengeance') is a prepositional phrase marking the manner/target of the coming, تو marks the target 'you', and آید (آمد#آ) is 'will come'. همان کینه‌ور follows the verb as a topical/identifying subject phrase ('that very vindictive one'); poetic word order places the subject after the verb for emphasis.
segments.s082-g67: بگفت is simple past 'he said' (the old preformative b‑), و links clauses. فرود آمد is a compound meaning 'came down' or 'fell down'. آبش is 'its/ his water' (آب + enclitic possessive ـش) and بروی here functions as a poetic contraction of بر روی/بر اوی meaning 'upon him / on his face' (an archaic locative expression), so the clause means 'and its water came down upon him.'
segments.s082-g68: همی is an intensifier here ('indeed/utterly' or progressive 'then'), زار bears the sense 'lamentingly / wretchedly' (used adverbially), and بگریست is past 'he wept'. نوذر is the personal name 'Nowzar' placed after the verb (poetic word order). The final بروی repeats the archaic locative 'before/at him' (here marked as an irregular/archaic form).
segments.s082-g69: بی‌آنکش is an adverbial phrase 'without that' (بی‑ + آنکش, an archaic demonstrative/particle). بدی here denotes 'harm/evil' used in the sense 'suffering' or 'harm done', followed by هیچ بیمار یی 'no illness' (هیچ negates بیماریی). The clause asserts lack of harm or disease under the given condition.
segments.s082-g70: نه 'nor' negates the second clause; از introduces the source/cause 'from' (i.e., 'from the pains'), دردها is the plural 'pains' or afflictions, هیچ again negates, and آزاریی (آزار) denotes 'harm/injury' — so 'nor any harm from the pains.'
segments.s082-g71: دو چشم کیانی is a quantified noun phrase 'the two Kayanian/royal eyes' (دو + noun + adjective/specifier). به هم is a reciprocal/collective adverbial 'together' or 'toward each other'. بر نهاد is the past form of نهادن with the prefix بر‑ meaning 'placed upon' or 'laid together' — here idiomatically 'brought/closed together'.
segments.s082-g72: بپژمرد is a past/formal verb 'they/it withered' (ب‑ preformative + پژمرد 'withered'). و 'and' links to the next action; برزد is an archaic past verb meaning 'came/struck' or 'fell upon'. یکی سرد باد is the noun phrase 'a cold wind' (poetic word order places verb before noun). Thus: 'they withered, and a cold wind came.'

sentences.s082-s37: That famed, highly skilled king passed away; his words remained in the world as a memorial of him.
sentences.s082-s5: If ever you must go to another world, may there be a place for you in the presence of God.
sentences.s082-s6: Consider what must now be done; one must not act so that charging brings death.
sentences.s082-s7: When the king heard the words from the knowledgeable one, he arranged the ceremony in a changed manner.
sentences.s082-s8: He summoned all the mobeds and the řadān, and laid bare all the secrets of his heart before them.
sentences.s082-s9: He commanded that Nowzar come before him, and gave him admonitions, warning against exceeding the proper measure.
sentences.s082-s10: for this royal throne is a bewitchment and may it not make the heart regard it as everlasting.
sentences.s082-s11: I reached one hundred and twenty years of age; I spent them amid toil and hardship.
sentences.s082-s12: I drove away much joy and the heart's desire; I remained amid the enemies in battle.
sentences.s082-s25: Then one will appear in the eastern land; look, so that you do not turn against him in hatred.
sentences.s082-s26: Give him that faith — it was of God; watch from the beginning what covenant there was.
sentences.s082-s27: Never abandon the divine way, for goodness is from Him and likewise evil is from Him.
sentences.s082-s28: After that a host will come from the Turks; they will place a cap upon the throne of Iran.
sentences.s082-s1: Manuchehr's years came to two sixties; from the world he bound up the burden of departing.
sentences.s082-s2: The astrologers turned their attention to him and began to tell tales drawn from the sky.
sentences.s082-s3: They did not see his day drawn out long; from the world he was again compelled to go.
sentences.s082-s4: On that bitter day they gave the news that the imperial throne had turned dark.
sentences.s082-s29: You have grave affairs ahead; sometimes a wolf must take on the body of a ewe.
sentences.s082-s30: Harm will come to you from the son (Pur) Pashang; because of Turan matters will become a shame upon you.
sentences.s082-s31: Seek, O son, when judgment comes; from Sam and Zal then seek aid.
sentences.s082-s32: And from this new tree that sprang from Zal's back it arose; now it lifts forth branch and mane.
sentences.s082-s21: A trace of you that will endure will reappear again in the long course of time.
sentences.s082-s22: There should be nothing except from the Creator, for pure birth brings a pure religion.
sentences.s082-s23: Look — so that you do not turn away from God's religion; for God's religion brings pure counsel.
sentences.s082-s24: Now (a) new judgment will arise in the world when Moses comes to prophethood.
sentences.s082-s13: I placed Fereydun's farr (divine glory) in the midst; by his counsel, every loss turned to my profit.
sentences.s082-s14: I withdrew from Salm and from Tur's gaze; it was that same hatred of Iraj, the great ancestor.
sentences.s082-s15: I set the world apart from the petty things (پتیاره‌ها); I founded many cities on many occasions.
sentences.s082-s16: So it is with me that, as if, I had not seen the world; the count has passed away into concealment.
sentences.s082-s17: His life is not worth the death of a tree that brings poison to its fruit and leaves.
sentences.s082-s18: After that — for I endured much pain and hardship — I entrusted to you the kingly throne and treasure.
sentences.s082-s19: Just as Fereydun had given it to me, I gave you this tested crown of kingship.
sentences.s082-s20: Take it thus: what befell you has passed, and you must be restored to a better time.
sentences.s082-s33: Because of him, the city of Turan will be left without skill; that very vindictive man will come upon you with vengeance.
sentences.s082-s34: He spoke, and its water came down upon him; Nowzar, lamenting bitterly, wept before him.
sentences.s082-s35: "Without that, you will suffer no illness, nor any harm from pains;"
sentences.s082-s36: He closed the two royal eyes together; they withered, and a cold wind came.
